title: Luhn-Valid BIN Permutation Attack with Sequential Card Number Iteration
narrative: Fraudster generates algorithmically valid card numbers by iterating through sequential PANs within a compromised BIN range, testing 40-120 Luhn-compliant variants within 90 minutes. Transactions cluster at $1.00-$4.99 to minimize alert thresholds while maximizing test volume. All attempts share identical P_emaildomain (often disposable providers like guerrillamail or tempmail), same addr1/dist1 pairing, and ProductCD='C' for digital goods. The attack signature shows card1 values incrementing by 1-3 digits while card2-card5 remain constant. Successful validations immediately followed by $200-$800 transactions within 15 minutes. CVV values rotate through common sequences (111, 123, 999) suggesting no actual CVV knowledge, relying purely on merchants with weak CVV enforcement or CNP fraud controls.
indicators: 40-120 unique card1 values within same IIN prefix (first 8 digits) attempted in 60-90 minute window
card1 values show sequential numeric progression (incrementing by 1-3) while card2-card5 hash values identical across attempts
Initial test transactions $1.00-$4.99 with 85%+ approval rate, followed by $200-$800 purchases within 15 minutes of successful validation
P_emaildomain matches disposable email pattern (tempmail, guerrillamail, 10minutemail) and shared across 30+ transaction attempts
CVV approval rate under 12% with rotating values 111/123/999/000 suggesting algorithmic guessing without actual CVV data
All attempts from same addr1/dist1 combination with ProductCD='C' or 'W', targeting digital goods merchants
card1_txn_count_1hr shows spike from 0 to 40+ for sequential card numbers never seen in historical data
summary: Sequential card numbers within the same BIN are tested rapidly with low-dollar amounts, sharing identical email domains, shipping details, and device fingerprints while CVV values cycle through obvious patterns like 111, 123, or 999. Successful test transactions are immediately followed by larger purchases on the validated cards within minutes.
reasoning: The attacker is systematically guessing complete card numbers by iterating through a stolen or leaked BIN range, using small transactions to identify which generated numbers are active accounts before quickly monetizing them with higher-value fraudulent purchases.
indicator_explanations: Dozens of different card numbers from same bank are being tested rapidly
Card numbers increment in sequence while everything else stays identical
Small test charges get approved then immediately followed by large fraudulent purchases
Same throwaway email address is being recycled across dozens of card attempts
CVV codes are being guessed with common patterns showing no real card data
All attempts originate from identical location targeting easy-to-monetize digital products
Brand new card numbers suddenly show massive transaction volume with no history
